title: Expression, purification, and characterization of VPS13C
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fwuty","text":"VPS13C is a ER-lysosome contact site protein that is thought to transport lipids between the two organellar membranes and important for lysosomal membrane damage repair. We employed structure-function analysis of purified VPS13C and uncovered novel regulatory mechanisms. This protocol details the method for expressing and purifying the human full-length VPS13C from Expi293F cells as well as structural characterization by negative-stain electron microscopy (EM). Procedures were similarly applied to the expression, purification, and in vitro characterization of other VPS13C mutants.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":537,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"amogf","text":"As the protein yield is low, handle procedures with care.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6g2ce","text":"Uranyl acetate solution is radioactive and requests special handling on a bench specifically for radioactive materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transfect 200 mL Expi293F cells at 2.4–2.8 × 10^6 cells/mL with 200 μg plasmid encoding full-length VPS13C or mutant constructs using Expifectamine. Add the manufacturer-supplied enhancers 18 h after transfection.
Harvest cells 48 h after transfection, flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at −80°C.
Thaw cells at room temperature and resuspend in 20 mL buffer A (500 mM NaCl, 50 mM HEPES pH 7.8, 10% glycerol, 1 mM TCEP) supplemented with 1× protease inhibitor cocktail.
Lyse cells by three freeze–thaw cycles between liquid nitrogen and a room-temperature water bath, then homogenize with a Dounce homogenizer.
Clarify the lysate by centrifugation at 27,143 × g for 30 min in a JA-20 rotor.
Incubate the supernatant with 100 μL anti-FLAG M2 resin for 2 h at 4°C.
Wash the resin twice with 15 mL buffer A, then incubate the resin overnight in buffer A supplemented with 1 mM freshly made ATP and 2 mM MgCl 2 to remove chaperones. 4 °C Overnight

Wash the resin twice more with buffer A and elute bound protein with buffer A containing 0.25 mg/mL FLAG peptide in five sequential 100 μL incubations of 20–30 min each. Pool the eluates. Purified VPS13C-3xFLAG

Load the pooled eluates onto a Superose 6 10/300 column pre-equilibrated with buffer B (200 mM NaCl, 50 mM HEPES pH 7.2, 6% glycerol, 1 mM TCEP). For structural analysis of full-length VPS13C, perform size-exclusion chromatography in buffer C (200 mM NaCl, 50 mM HEPES pH 7.2, 1 mM TCEP), then collect and concentrate peak fractions.
Perform particle picking and 2D classification in CryoSPARC.

Acquire images on an FEI Tecnai T12 transmission electron microscope at 120 kV and 52,000× nominal magnification, corresponding to 2.14 Å/pixel at the specimen level. Representative negative stain EM image
Blot the sample and stain with three to five sequential 5 μL drops of 2% uranyl acetate, allowing the final drop to incubate for 30 s before blotting to dryness.
Apply 5–7 μL protein sample diluted to 50-100 nanomolar (nM) to the grid and incubate for 30 s.
Glow-discharge 400-mesh carbon-coated copper grids for 35 s at 25 mA.
Culture Expi293F cells in 8% CO 2 with orbital shaking at 125 rpm, following the manufacturer’s instructions (Thermo Scientific, MAN0007814).
creator: Dazhi Li
